lines.english: Lesson 22
lines.chinese: 第22课
lines.raw: Lesson 22 | 第22课

lines.english: By heart
lines.chinese: 熟记于心
lines.raw: By heart | 熟记于心

lines.english: Which actor read the letter in the end, the aristocrat or the gaoler?
lines.chinese: 最终是哪位演员读了信，是贵族还是狱卒？
lines.raw: Which actor read the letter in the end, the aristocrat or the gaoler? | 最终是哪位演员读了信，是贵族还是狱卒？

lines.english: Some plays are so successful that they run for years on end.
lines.chinese: 有些戏剧非常成功，一连上演好几年。
lines.raw: Some plays are so successful that they run for years on end. | 有些戏剧非常成功，一连上演好几年。

lines.english: In many ways, this is unfortunate for the poor actors who are required to go on repeating the same lines night after night.
lines.chinese: 在许多方面，这对可怜的演员来说是不幸的，他们被要求夜复一夜地重复相同的台词。
lines.raw: In many ways, this is unfortunate for the poor actors who are required to go on repeating the same lines night after night. | 在许多方面，这对可怜的演员来说是不幸的，他们被要求夜复一夜地重复相同的台词。

lines.english: One would expect them to know their parts by heart and never have cause to falter.
lines.chinese: 人们会期望他们熟记自己的角色台词，永不结巴。
lines.raw: One would expect them to know their parts by heart and never have cause to falter. | 人们会期望他们熟记自己的角色台词，永不结巴。

lines.english: Yet this is not always the case.
lines.chinese: 然而，事实并非总是如此。
lines.raw: Yet this is not always the case. | 然而，事实并非总是如此。

lines.english: A famous actor in a highly successful play was once cast in the role of an aristocrat who had been imprisoned in the Bastille for twenty years.
lines.chinese: 一部非常成功的戏剧中，一位著名演员曾被塑造成一位在巴士底狱被囚禁了二十年的贵族。
lines.raw: A famous actor in a highly successful play was once cast in the role of an aristocrat who had been imprisoned in the Bastille for twenty years. | 一部非常成功的戏剧中，一位著名演员曾被塑造成一位在巴士底狱被囚禁了二十年的贵族。

lines.english: In the last act, a gaoler would always come on to the stage with a letter which he would hand to the prisoner.
lines.chinese: 在最后一幕中，一个狱卒总是会带着一封信上台，然后递给囚犯。
lines.raw: In the last act, a gaoler would always come on to the stage with a letter which he would hand to the prisoner. | 在最后一幕中，一个狱卒总是会带着一封信上台，然后递给囚犯。

lines.english: Even though the noble was expected to read the letter at each performance, he always insisted that it should be written out in full.
lines.chinese: 尽管这位贵族在每次表演中都应该读这封信，但他总是坚持信应该写得清清楚楚。
lines.raw: Even though the noble was expected to read the letter at each performance, he always insisted that it should be written out in full. | 尽管这位贵族在每次表演中都应该读这封信，但他总是坚持信应该写得清清楚楚。

lines.english: One night, the gaoler decided to play a joke on his colleague to find out if,
lines.chinese: 一天晚上，狱卒决定和他的同事开个玩笑，想看看，
lines.raw: One night, the gaoler decided to play a joke on his colleague to find out if, | 一天晚上，狱卒决定和他的同事开个玩笑，想看看，

lines.english: after so many performances, he had managed to learn the contents of the letter by heart.
lines.chinese: 在这么多场演出之后，他是否已经把信的内容熟记于心。
lines.raw: after so many performances, he had managed to learn the contents of the letter by heart. | 在这么多场演出之后，他是否已经把信的内容熟记于心。

lines.english: The curtain went up on the final act of the play and revealed the aristocrat sitting alone behind bars in his dark cell.
lines.chinese: 戏剧的最后一幕拉开了帷幕，露出了贵族独自一人坐在漆黑牢房的铁窗后面。
lines.raw: The curtain went up on the final act of the play and revealed the aristocrat sitting alone behind bars in his dark cell. | 戏剧的最后一幕拉开了帷幕，露出了贵族独自一人坐在漆黑牢房的铁窗后面。

lines.english: Just then, the gaoler appeared with the precious letter in his hands.
lines.chinese: 就在那时，狱卒手里拿着那封珍贵的信出现了。
lines.raw: Just then, the gaoler appeared with the precious letter in his hands. | 就在那时，狱卒手里拿着那封珍贵的信出现了。

lines.english: He entered the cell and presented the letter to the aristocrat.
lines.chinese: 他走进牢房，把信递给了贵族。
lines.raw: He entered the cell and presented the letter to the aristocrat. | 他走进牢房，把信递给了贵族。

lines.english: But the copy he gave him had not been written out in full as usual.
lines.chinese: 但他给他的那份信件并未像往常一样完整写出。
lines.raw: But the copy he gave him had not been written out in full as usual. | 但他给他的那份信件并未像往常一样完整写出。

lines.english: It was simply a blank sheet of paper.
lines.chinese: 那只是一张白纸。
lines.raw: It was simply a blank sheet of paper. | 那只是一张白纸。

lines.english: The gaoler looked on eagerly, anxious to see if his fellow actor had at last learnt his lines.
lines.chinese: 狱卒焦急地看着，渴望知道他的同事演员是否终于记住了台词。
lines.raw: The gaoler looked on eagerly, anxious to see if his fellow actor had at last learnt his lines. | 狱卒焦急地看着，渴望知道他的同事演员是否终于记住了台词。

lines.english: The noble stared at the blank sheet of paper for a few seconds.
lines.chinese: 贵族盯着那张白纸看了几秒钟。
lines.raw: The noble stared at the blank sheet of paper for a few seconds. | 贵族盯着那张白纸看了几秒钟。

lines.english: Then, squinting his eyes, he said: 'The light is dim. Read the letter to me.'
lines.chinese: 然后，他眯着眼睛说：“光线太暗了。你把信念给我听吧。”
lines.raw: Then, squinting his eyes, he said: 'The light is dim. Read the letter to me.' | 然后，他眯着眼睛说：“光线太暗了。你把信念给我听吧。”

lines.english: And he promptly handed the sheet of paper to the gaoler.
lines.chinese: 他立刻把那张纸递给了狱卒。
lines.raw: And he promptly handed the sheet of paper to the gaoler. | 他立刻把那张纸递给了狱卒。

lines.english: Finding that he could not remember a word of the letter either,
lines.chinese: 狱卒发现自己也记不住信里一个字，
lines.raw: Finding that he could not remember a word of the letter either, | 狱卒发现自己也记不住信里一个字，

lines.english: the gaoler replied: 'The light is indeed dim, sire. I must get my glasses. '
lines.chinese: 他回答说：“陛下，光线确实很暗。我必须去拿我的眼镜。”
lines.raw: the gaoler replied: 'The light is indeed dim, sire. I must get my glasses. ' | 他回答说：“陛下，光线确实很暗。我必须去拿我的眼镜。”

lines.english: With this, he hurried off the stage.
lines.chinese: 说完，他匆匆忙忙地下了台。
lines.raw: With this, he hurried off the stage. | 说完，他匆匆忙忙地下了台。

lines.english: Much to the aristocrat's amusement,
lines.chinese: 令贵族非常有趣的是，
lines.raw: Much to the aristocrat's amusement, | 令贵族非常有趣的是，

lines.english: the gaoler returned a few moments later with a pair of glasses and the usual copy of the letter which he proceeded to read to the prisoner.
lines.chinese: 狱卒几分钟后带着一副眼镜和往常那封信的副本回来了，然后他开始念给囚犯听。
lines.raw: the gaoler returned a few moments later with a pair of glasses and the usual copy of the letter which he proceeded to read to the prisoner. | 狱卒几分钟后带着一副眼镜和往常那封信的副本回来了，然后他开始念给囚犯听。
